page: Microsoft Azure E16s_v5 is an ESv5 family virtual machine configured with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz, this x86_64 architecture server features 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, resulting in 2 threads per core. The processor includes a 48 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration via AES and SHA_NI.

This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external Premium Storage options. Additionally, it provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core positions this hardware class as a memory-optimized resource, trading off local storage and hardware acceleration for substantial memory density.

Benchmark results place the E16s_v5 in the average performance tier across most standardized tests. It achieved a Geekbench multi-core score of 10035.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 21180.44. Memory bandwidth tests show read-only speeds of 834.31 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 102.57 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Notably, the server demonstrates strong performance in text generation for small language models, reaching 295.71 tokens/sec, while maintaining average speeds of 15.7 tokens/sec for medium models.

Given its memory-to-vCPU ratio and average database operations score of 7997.58, the E16s_v5 is suited for memory-intensive workloads such as relational databases, caching layers like Redis, and medium-scale enterprise applications. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the lack of local storage and GPU hardware reduces overhead for workloads that rely strictly on high memory capacity and remote storage volumes.

Built on the x86_64 architecture with 8 physical cores and hyper-threading, it includes a 48 MB L3 cache and supports AVX-512 instructions. This server does not bundle local storage or GPUs, relying instead on remote Premium Storage. Benchmarks show average performance across most tests, including a Geekbench multi-core score of 10035.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 21180.44, alongside a strong text generation speed of 295.71 tokens/sec for small language models. With a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core, this instance is designed for memory-intensive workloads such as database operations, caching layers, and enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E16s_v5 is an ESv5 virtual machine with 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and no local storage, delivering average multi-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.

tagline: An Intel-powered, memory-optimized virtual machine with 128 GB RAM and 16 dedicated vCPUs for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor at 2.8 GHz
128.0 GB of system memory providing a high 8 GB per core ratio
No local storage or integrated GPUs, requiring remote Premium Storage
Strong text generation performance of 295.71 tokens/sec for small LLMs
Suited for database operations, Redis caching, and memory-heavy workloads
